title: Agalmyla chorisepala
intro: Agalmyla chorisepala, also known as the "Hairy-flowered Agalmyla", is a species of flowering plant in the family Gesneriaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of various ailments such as wounds, skin infections, and respiratory issues. Bioactive constituents of Agalmyla chorisepala include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are thought to contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from Agalmyla chorisepala include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to harness its medicinal benefits and promote overall well-being.

Further investigation is required to confirm its medicinal uses.
